title: Maa Baulasuni Temple
tagline: Maa Baulasuni Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Maa Baulasuni Temple is located in the village of Nilok in Bhadrak District, at about a distance of 27 km from the city of Bhadrak.
overview.description: Maa Baulasuni Temple is located in the village of Nilok in Bhadrak District, at about a distance of 27 km from the city of Bhadrak. At first there was no temple, the Idol was worshiped under a tree. Then an altar was made where the Idol of Maa Baulasuni placed for worship. The present temple was built on 1969 and the "Yagya Mandap" was built on the year of 2000.

topPlaces.name: Nilok
topPlaces.description: Nilok is a village located in Bonth Block of Bhadrak district in the Indian state of Odisha. It is situated 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) away from sub-district headquarters Bant and 28 km (17 mi) away from district headquarters Bhadrak.

items.note: Nilok is a village located in Bonth Block of Bhadrak district in the Indian state of Odisha. It is situated 4 kilometres (2.5 mi) away from sub-district headquarters Bant and 28 km (17 mi) away from district headquarters Bhadrak.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maa Baulasuni Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maa Baulasuni Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maa Baulasuni Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (108 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (105 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maa Baulasuni Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maa Baulasuni Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maa Baulasuni Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Maa Baulasuni Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
